source_evidence: Eq. (2)-(3): min over s+,s- of V = l^T (s+/sigma_T + s-/sigma_C) s.t. B^T(s+ - s-) = f, s+_i,s-_i >= 0; with a_i = s+_i/sigma_T + s-_i/sigma_C and n_i = s+_i - s-_i. Rewritten with kappa = sigma_T/sigma_C as V* = V/sigma_T = {l^T kappa l^T}{s+;s-}.
purpose: Reformulates the plastic minimum-volume truss problem as a linear program by introducing non-negative tension/compression slack variables, enabling efficient solution via interior-point methods.
assumptions: Member is in tension if s+ > 0, compression if s- > 0
Stress constraint must be active for every member at the optimum
If truss is stable with no repeated/overlapping members, rank of B^T is N_dof so solution lies on a corner of the feasible domain
At most N_dof non-zero basic variables at optimum; remainder absent
Optimal volume computed for sigma_T = 1 and scaled by 1/sigma_T otherwise
Number of design variables doubled relative to the nonlinear form
formula_or_algorithm: Define slack variables s+ (tension) and s- (compression). Objective: min l^T(s+/sigma_T + s-/sigma_C). Constraint: [B^T  -B^T]{s+; s-} = f with s+,s- >= 0. Recover a_i = s+_i/sigma_T + s-_i/sigma_C and n_i = s+_i - s-_i. Using kappa = sigma_T/sigma_C gives V* = V/sigma_T. Solve as a linear program (interior-point / Karmarkar). Degenerate solutions lie on a facet/edge and are harder to solve.
validation_opportunities: benchmark against nonlinear formulation (1) giving same optimum
corner-solution check (at most N_dof non-zero basic variables)

reuse_risk.explanation: Standard, well-posed linear program with an explicit reformulation and recovery mapping; solvable with off-the-shelf LP solvers.

missing_information: Explicit construction of the doubled constraint matrix in code
Cutoff threshold used to prune small-area members
